releases.name: Vatican (DDF/DCE) — Antiqua et Nova (2025)
releases.rationale: First faith institution in the corpus, and the segment gap is the point: Antiqua et Nova reasons to the same line from entirely different premises. The Vatican does not cite verification economics; it says only a human is truly a moral agent - the accountability half of the pattern derived from theology instead of publishing ethics. When an institution that answers to doctrine rather than peer review draws the line the BBC and Springer Nature drew, the pattern's independence claim gets its strongest test yet.

releases.name: USPTO — Inventorship Guidance for AI-Assisted Inventions
releases.rationale: USPTO — Inventorship Guidance for AI-Assisted Inventions is the strongest coded-but-unreleased record in the queue (advisory 8/10: recognition 3 · segment gap 2 · theory 1 · timeliness 2). CA 8/12 with a Balanced posture; it opens the regulator segment — no released institution covers it yet; its policy independently fits the verification-boundary pattern, adding replication weight. In the policy's own words: "Only a natural person can be an inventor, so AI cannot be.". Coded 8 day(s) ago.

releases.name: QAA (UK Quality Assurance Agency)

releases.name: Science / AAAS
releases.rationale: Science / AAAS changed its policy and the change is re-coded: VOCABULARY (NO-SUBSTANTIVE-CHANGE), CA 10 -> 10, revision 1, observed 2026-07-12. Previously coded sentence 'An AI program cannot be an author of a Science journal paper' no longer appears; the AI authorship clause is rewritten and expanded (no AI authors or AI-authored cited sourc…. A live institution's policy moving is the strongest beat the Index logs — the longitudinal record is the point.
